name: manager-planner
description: Orchestrates Pukaist agents, enforces plan-first workflow, runs integrity tests, and delegates tasks; use for coordination or system audits.
capabilities: Orchestrates 6 specialized Pukaist agents (Gatekeeper, Analyst, Scribe, Archivist, Historian, Barrister)
Enforces plan-first workflow requiring user approval before any task execution
Runs automated integrity tests to verify system health and stability
Performs system audits checking log consistency, queue health, and output validation
Delegates tasks to specific agents based on workflow requirements
Maintains quality control standards including verbatim citation and neutral voice requirements
author: ACSKamloops
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate workflow orchestration system for document processing. Scanner flagged SHA256 hashes as C2/weak crypto (they are secure hashes for file integrity), and documentation code examples in SKILL.md as command execution (they are markdown references, not code). No malicious patterns confirmed.
valueStatement: Managing complex multi-agent projects requires coordination across team members. This skill orchestrates Pukaist agents with a strict plan-first workflow, ensuring every task is approved before execution and maintaining system integrity through automated testing.
userTitle: Coordinate Agent Workflows with Plan-First Approach
limitations: Designed specifically for Pukaist system integration, may not work with other agent frameworks
Requires the full Pukaist agent ecosystem to function (6 specialized agents)
Cannot execute tasks without a user-approved plan; always requires human approval
Limited to document processing workflows; not a general-purpose project management tool

useCases.targetUser: Legal operations teams managing high-volume document review projects
useCases.title: Manage Document Review Pipelines
useCases.description: Coordinate document ingestion, OCR processing, evidence extraction, and legal brief synthesis across multiple specialized agents with quality checkpoints.

useCases.targetUser: System administrators overseeing automated document processing systems
useCases.title: System Health Monitoring
useCases.description: Run comprehensive system audits that verify queue integrity, log consistency, and output quality across all active agents in the workflow.

useCases.targetUser: Project managers coordinating cross-functional teams with defined workflows
useCases.title: Multi-Phase Project Coordination
useCases.description: Break down complex projects into atomic steps, present plans for approval, and delegate to specialized agents while tracking progress through the pipeline.

promptTemplates.title: Start New Document Review
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to process a new batch of documents through the review pipeline. Start by assessing the current system state, then present a plan for me to approve.
promptTemplates.scenario: Initial document batch intake and workflow initiation

promptTemplates.title: Run System Health Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Check the system health. Run the integrity tests and health check commands. Report any issues found and suggest fixes.
promptTemplates.scenario: Routine system maintenance and stability verification

promptTemplates.title: Audit Agent Outputs
promptTemplates.prompt: Perform a deep audit of recent agent outputs. Check that all files have valid citations, neutral language, and proper provenance logging.
promptTemplates.scenario: Quality assurance review of completed work

promptTemplates.title: Delegate Complex Task
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to complete a complex multi-step task. Please break it down into atomic steps with the appropriate agent assignments, then wait for my approval before proceeding.

bestPractices: Always wait for user approval before delegating tasks; the plan-first approach prevents wasted work and ensures alignment.
Run integrity tests before major operations to ensure the system is stable and ready to process tasks.
Use bounded snapshots and targeted reads rather than dumping entire documents to maintain efficiency.
antiPatterns: Never start implementing a task immediately without presenting a plan first; this violates the core workflow principle.
Do not rely on a single sample when validating outputs; sample multiple files across different categories.
Avoid letting tasks stall in InProgress status for more than 24 hours without intervention.

faq.question: What is the Pukaist system?
faq.answer: Pukaist is a multi-agent document processing system with 6 specialized agents. This skill coordinates those agents to ensure proper workflow execution.

faq.question: Can I skip the planning phase?
faq.answer: No. The plan-first approach is a core design principle. You must always draft and get approval before any agent executes tasks.

faq.question: What happens if integrity tests fail?
faq.answer: If any test fails, you must stop all operations and fix the underlying issue before proceeding with any task execution.

faq.question: How do I add new agents to the system?
faq.answer: This skill manages the existing 6 Pukaist agents. Adding new agents would require updates to the agent roster in the skill documentation.

faq.question: What quality standards are enforced?
faq.answer: All outputs must have valid citations, neutral language (no opinions), and verifiable provenance. This ensures legal-grade documentation.

faq.question: How do I recover from a stalled queue?
faq.answer: Check Flagged_Tasks.tsv for errors. For items stuck in InProgress, review the Agent_Communication_Log to identify the blocker, then take corrective action.
